meaning: n.闅旂粷,瀛ょ珛,闅旂,缁濈紭,绂绘瀽
examples: They told me about isolation and public apathy.
To make sense, these figures should not be looked at in isolation.
Hayley contracted tuberculosis and had to be put in an isolation ward.
Part of the appeal of the often-bleak Maumturks is in their wild, unchanging isolation.
No-cost mobility reduces social isolation and provides a lifeline to vital social services for older Londoners.
